pulse: The healthcare sector currently trades at a median EV/EBITDA of 13.7x and a median P/E of 25.5x, slightly above the broader market multiples, indicating robust investor interest. A significant observation is Eli Lilly (LLY) with an EV/EBITDA of 30.8x and a trailing P/E of 45.9x, reflecting its 42.6% revenue growth and recently highlighted achievements, reiterated by headlines noting its $1 trillion market cap, suggesting strong confidence in its growth trajectory. Looking ahead, as earnings in pharma outpace the S&P 500 per Bloomberg's coverage, investors should consider positioning in names like AstraZeneca (AZN) and Merck KGaA (MRK), which are capitalizing on strong drug demand following impressive quarterly earnings reports.
